description.en: A globally beloved companion animal that perfectly retains the deadly agility, sharp senses, and hunting instincts of a wild predator.
description.zh: 一种在全球备受人类喜爱的伴生动物，完美地保留了作为野生微型捕食者那致命的敏捷性、敏锐的感官和狩猎本能。

anatomy.en: Boasting an incredibly flexible spine and a 'righting reflex', cats can twist their bodies mid-air to almost always land gracefully on their feet. Their tongues feature tiny backwards-facing barbs made of keratin, acting like a built-in comb.
anatomy.zh: 凭借着极其柔软灵活的脊柱和神奇的「翻正反射」，猫咪竟能在半空中扭转身体，从而几乎总能优雅且平稳地四足着地。它们的舌头上还布满了由角蛋白组成的朝后的微小倒刺，就像一把自带的梳子。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Despite domesticated life, cats are hypercarnivores (requiring a diet mostly of meat) and rely on deep, stealthy instincts. Meaningful 'meowing' is surprisingly a behavior they developed primarily to communicate with humans, not with each other!
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 尽管已经过上了被人类驯化的生活，但猫咪依然是绝对的「超级肉食动物」（饮食必须以肉类为主），并依赖着深深刻在骨子里的潜行本能。令人惊讶的是，它们那充满含义的「喵喵叫」，主要是为了和人类沟通而进化出来的行为，而不是用来借此与同类交流的！

habitat_and_distribution.en: Originating from Near Eastern wildcats, they now live on every continent except Antarctica. Their true habitat is simply 'wherever humans are'—from high-rise city apartments to isolated rural barns.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 它们起源于近东野猫，现在已经在除了南极洲以外的每个大洲都安了家。它们真正的栖息地其实就是「有人类存在的地方」——从城市里的摩天大楼公寓，一直到与世隔绝的乡村谷仓。
